TextENGNew: ``--What did you say?!@k@vS16/00/DS43040203. The bridge has collapsed?!``
changeReason: Old version translates なんだって as a simple exclamation '...What?!' while new version captures the full meaning 'What did you say?!' which is closer to the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Y-yes!! At this rate, it will be impossible to evacuate to the neighboring village!!``
Hyougo, gasping for breath as he ran to Mao, delivered the report.
He had been at the front of the evacuation alongside Azuma, guiding the villagers, but the unexpected emergency had sent him racing back.
``This is no joke... This is the only route left to escape the village, isn't it?!``
``Wh-what do we do, Mao-sama?! At this rate, everyone will...``
``Calm down! You're proud to be the bravest of our bunch, yeah?!@k@vS16/00/DS43040206. Then now's not the time for bitching!``
Mao shouted that... but deep down, she was struggling to hide her own panic.
Onigafuchi Village had been nearly wiped out from a demon attack 25 years ago... back when Mao was a little girl and not a member of the village yet.
Back when she'd heard about it from the elders, she snorted and said she'd exterminate the demon herself...
But now, she'd already thrown away such appearances and pride, and it was taking her all her power to gather the villagers fleeing in fear.
``If we stay stranded here, it'll catch up to us...!@k@vS16/00/DS43040208. Split up and look for any byroads near the mountains! Even an animal trail will do!``
``Y-yes ma'am!``
...Just then, there was a shout from up ahead, and the maelstrom of screams rapidly worsened.
``What's wrong?! What was that voice?!!``
``I-it seems someone jumped to their death!! Everyone's losing their minds in fear!!``
``Kh...! I'll be fine.@k@vS16/00/DS43040211. You do what you can to calm down the people up front!@k@vS16/00/DS43040212. It'll be over for real if we all lose our heads!``
``Y-yes ma'am!``
``Everyone, stop!@k@vS16/00/DS43040214. It's a dead end up ahead! Wait a little while until we find another route!!``
``No, that can't be...!!``
``Y-you're kidding me! What're we gonna do if the demon catches up to us?!``
``W-we're doomed...! We're all gonna die...!``
The villagers all wailed as they broke the line and tried to go forward.
Mao desperately comforted them, encouraged them... and occasionally ordered her subordinates to resort to violence to quell the chaos.
``Calm down and listen up!@k@vS16/00/DS43040216. You call yourselves the people of Onigafuchi?! The village that already survived a demon attack?!``
``When you're killed by a demon, the only difference is if it comes sooner or later!@k@vS16/00/DS43040218. Get a grip and believe in your own destiny!!``
``M-Mao-san...!``
(...I wanna run away just as much as the rest of them.)
Mao thought that to herself as she yelled... and looked down at her young son, who was fearfully clinging to her kimono.
...If it means my boy can be saved...

It wasn't like she hadn't had cowardly thoughts about doing something inhuman to save him... like sacrificing the entire village to attract the attention of the demon and finding another way to escape in the meantime.
But... even if they did survive by some chance, what would she tell her son?
Admit she'd committed the heinous and shameless act of sacrificing many to survive?
Then how could she ever question the morality of her son in the future when she'd given in to such temptation?
(Children grow by watching their parents. One who commits evil has no right teaching virtue...!)
That dignity of hers' as a mother was the one thing barely keeping her reasoning and endurance tied together.
``M-Mao-san!@k@vS16/00/DS43030406. Is Mao-san there?!``
Just then, Shino, who was supposed to be escorting the villagers fleeing in the rear, frantically came running over.
She'd probably come to ask what they were going to do about the collapsed bridge... but it wasn't like Mao had any ideas either.
So Mao turned around annoyed and let her irritation get the better of her before she even heard Shino.
``What is it, Shino?!@k@vS16/00/DS43040222. Save it for later!``
``T-trouble... We've got big trouble!!``

``Yeah, I know, the bridge is out!@k@vS16/00/DS43040224. But how's the mayor supposed to instruct the villagers when she's panicking?!@k@vS16/00/DS43040225. Anyway, we're looking for another way out, so go back and calm down the oth...``
``Has Ouka... Has Ouka come this way?``
Mao, who'd been trying to drive away the prattling Shino, stopped thinking the moment she heard that unexpected question. What Shino had come to ask about wasn't the bridge, but Ouka's whereabouts.
``I-I haven't seen her around here...@k@vS16/00/DS43040228. Isn't she with your group...?``
``Do you think I'd come all this way to ask if she were?!@k@vS16/00/DS43030410. I figured she might have regrouped with you, so...!``
Even Mao froze and widened her eyes in shock when she heard that.
Ouka wasn't with either of them...?
She had a strong sense of responsibility, so she should've been helping lead the evacuation.
So both Shino and Mao assumed she was doing some kind of work in the other's group.
And yet... she wasn't?!
``?! O-Oukaaaa!!@k@vS16/00/DS43040231. Fuuka! Shouji! Anyone! Answer me if you're there!!``
``I already looked!@k@vS16/00/DS43030412. I split up the two groups to check, and checked myself over and over again!@k@vS16/00/DS43030413. But she's nowhere to be found!!``
``N-no, don't tell me...?!``
...The worst possible scenario came to Mao's mind.
She'd been panicking when they evacuated. She'd been entirely focused on leading the villagers and escaping.
And so... that's why...!
How careless she was to assume her dear friend was okay and not confirm her safety!!
``L-let's take another hard look!@k@vS16/00/DS43040234. Maybe you just overlooked her or someth...``
Just then...
``O-Ouka-sama is... a-at Furude Shrine...!``
A trembling young man fearfully confessed the truth to the both of them.
...Though they were so shocked they were stupefied, they carefully listened to every word he had to say.
It was then that they first learned that some of the villagers had gone on a rampage searching for the location of the medicine... and intruded on Furude Shrine to harm Ouka and her family.
Then... this was Oyashiro-sama's curse?
Was he saying that a demon had attacked this village once again... because the villagers had forgotten they owed their lives to Oyashiro-sama, and forsaken him in their by greed?!
Then did that mean Ouka, who served Oyashiro-sama as the representative of the villagers... was the one who took the brunt of Oyashiro-sama's wrath...?!
``Why... Why didn't you mention this sooner?!!``
``I-I tried to stop them! I told them Oyashiro-sama would curse us! B-but they said they'd kill me if I opposed them, so...!``
``N-no... Ouka, Oukaaaa!!``
``...! Wait, Shino-san!!``
Shino darted around and tried going back the way she came, pushing her way through the crowd.
Mao quickly caught up to her and restrained her with the help of several nearby villagers.
``L-let me go!@k@vS16/00/DS43030417. I'll bring Ouka... I'll bring Ouka and her family here!``
``Shino-san, don't!@k@vS16/00/DS43040239. There's a sea of fire waiting back there! You'll die too!!``
``Then what about Ouka?@k@vS16/00/DS43030419. Are you telling me to abandon her?!``
When she heard the word abandon... Mao gasped, feeling like her heart had been gouged out.
...Like hell.
Like hell I'd want to abandon my best friend... the girl I care so much for.
That kind girl who accepted me when I was lonely after the rest of the villagers rejected me.
She helped me get back on my feet when I fell into despair after my husband's death.
She stuck up for me just the other day about the theft, even though it meant defying her mother-in-law...
She's my best friend... My sister... The one I trusted most!
I want to go save her too!
I want to go back to the village and save her and her family, even if it costs me my life!
I can't abandon her...! No way in hell!!
But...
But now... it's too late...!
``Shino-san... No, Mayor.@k@vS16/00/DS43040243. You'll never make it in time even if you go back now.@k@vS16/00/DS43040244. ...Please understand...!``
``What?! Mao-san!!``
``Shino-san!!``
There was a loud creak.
Mao gritted her teeth so hard she nearly shattered them... as she forced herself to speak.
This wasn't... her personal feelings speaking.
This was a bitter decision she'd made as the head of the Sonozaki family, one of the Three Families who held responsibility for the entire village.
``You and I are members of the Three Families who command this village!@k@vS16/00/DS43040247. So it's our duty and responsibility to protect these villagers!``
``I understand that even though I'm young and inexperienced, so surely you must too!!``
``Don't give me that!@k@vS16/00/DS43030423. It doesn't matter if I'm the mayor or a member of the Three Families!!@k@vS16/00/DS43030424. Ouka... Ouka and her family are my own dear family!``
``What's so wrong about wanting to protect your family first and foremost?!``
``Shino-san...!``

``I made a promise...!@k@vS16/00/DS43030427. My beloved friends... Riku and Hanyuu entrusted her future to me!!@k@vS16/00/DS43030428. I swore before their graves I would see to it that she remained happy until the very end!!``
That was... the least Shino felt she could do to atone to them.
Since she couldn't protect her parents until the very end, taking care of Ouka for life was her penance.
She knew... she knew full well that what Mao said was absolutely right!
But how could she ever face Riku and Hanyuu if she abandoned this duty?
What excuse could she make?
That was what mayors were for to begin with. So Shino couldn't afford to budge on that...!

``I'll never abandon those I love... Never again!@k@vS16/00/DS43030430. If it means abandoning someone again, then I'd rather die with them!@k@vS16/00/DS43030431. So move... move aside!!@k@vS16/00/DS43030432. Out of my waaaaaaaay!!``
Mao bit her lip as she stomped up to Shino, who'd abandoned her normal composure and fallen into a frenzy.
Then she stood in front of that woman who was trying to shake off her subordinates' grips, and...
``I'm sorry...``
Mao punched her hard in the solar plexus.
...Shino groaned from that unexpected blow to her vitals and passed out, crumbling to the ground.
``M-Mao-sama...``
``...Carry Shino-san.``
``B-but...! At this rate, once Shino-san wakes up, she'll...``
``Have my head, I know.@k@vS16/00/DS43040254. ...But she's necessary for Onigafuchi Village's future.``
Mao said that, bowed to Shino, and turned around. Then she picked up her son and kept moving forward.
...If this hell was Oyashiro-sama's curse, there's nothing we can do about it. Our only choice is to accept it.
But... but that doesn't mean we can fall into despair... Absolutely not!
It's my duty to protect the village and the villagers Ouka loved and tried so hard to protect!
I'll gladly let the world call me a cold-hearted bitch so long as I can fulfill that duty...!
``...That's right. I'll create the future of this aftermath...!``
That was the least Mao could do to repay her close friend.
And... it would be her atonement...
She somehow managed to parry the full-powered claw strike.
...But the blow was so ferocious that Ouka's small body couldn't take it, and was blown away like a wood chip.
``Ughhhh...!!``
Ouka fiercely crashed into a wrecked building and somersaulted on the ground.
She tried to get up on instinct... but her legs wouldn't listen to her.
No, that was far from her worst problem...
``Gaghhh...?!``
She hacked up something from inside her chest.
...It was a spray of fresh crimson blood.
It was from her suddenly labored breathing, her dimming line of sight, and the accompanying dizziness, that Ouka realized she'd broken her ribs and damaged her lungs.
``Agh, aghhhh....!!``
Despite that, she desperately staggered to her feet and took a deep breath... Her rib cage swelled with the air, and fortunately dislodged its tip from her lung.
``Haah, haah, haaah...!!``
Ouka's sense of astonishment won out over her fear as she heaved her bloodstained breath, and turned back to face the ``demon`` in front of her baring its fangs.
(So this is... a ``demon's`` power...?)
They were different. They were far too different... Its power, speed, and skill were on a whole different level.
It had literally only suffered damage at the very start... They'd exchanged dozens of sword strikes since, but she'd never managed to strike the ``demon`` once.
She'd called it a ``monster`` with the intention of saying farewell, but now she could find no better expression to describe the overwhelming advantage it possessed.
Grrrrr... Grrrrr...
The ``demon`` let out a growl devoid of all reason, as it slowly closed the gap between itself and Ouka.
Ouka thrust out her sword and encouraged her aching body as she turned toward the sharp glare and waited for an attack.
Gaaaaaaaaaagh!!

Ouka couldn't help but mutter that when faced with the creature's horrifying twisted form distorted beyond all recognition, which the ``demon`` saw as its chance to kick off the ground and charge at her.
It raised its arm and brought down its claws in one swift motion.
``Yaaaaaaaaaaaaah!!!``
Ouka twisted her body out of the way in the nick of time and used the recoil to swing her sword to the side.
...But even though the ``demon`` was but a hair's breadth away, it barely dodged the blow and let out a howl not of this world.
Gaaaaaaaaaaaaagh!!!
It was a bloodcurdling roar that felt as though it could crush a heart.
Ouka flinched when she heard that from up close, but felt a bloodthirsty blade on her shoulder right after, and immediately threw herself forward.
...At that moment, a shockwave that seemed to cut through the wind was released, followed by a searing heat that enveloped Ouka's shoulder.
The wet sensation turned into numbness, and her left arm quickly became as heavy as lead.
Ouka tried to fill her dangling arm with strength to lift it, but that only served to wrack her with pain so intense that she dropped her sword and held her shoulder.
...Blood gushed out to the beat of her heart.
It stained her left sleeve red and caused the fabric to stubbornly cling to her skin.
Her vision grew darker and darker in tandem with that, nearly blinding her entirely.
Her consciousness was fading.
Her legs lost their strength.
Ouka collapsed to the ground... falling to her knees, but barely managing to raise her head to look forward.
Grrrrrrrrrr... Grrrrrrrrr...!!
...The ``demon`` still wasn't letting up the deadly shockwaves emanating from its body.
Even though it was bleeding heavily from the wound it got before it went berserk... its breath was ragged yet fierce, while its face was distorted yet not in pain.
(Am I really... no match...?)
Ghhh, gwooooooh...!
``I won't kill you.@k@vS16/45/DS43071931. Not here, not yet...!``
And then...
The ``demon`` spoke like a human for the first time, as it arrogantly approached Ouka step by step.

``You are no longer my friend.@k@vS16/45/DS43071933. ...Least of all my daughter.@k@vS16/45/DS43071934. You are merely... my enemy.@k@vS16/45/DS43071935. A worthless and trifling sacrifice that only exists to be hunted and slain by me...!!``

``But even death is not enough to atone for the sin of betraying me and calling me a monster.@k@vS16/45/DS43071937. ...As such, I will first tear, crush, and destroy everything you hold dear, and only then will I kill you...!``

``It is my wish that you shiver and lament in regret and agony worse than death in the short time before I kill you.@k@vS16/45/DS43071939. ...You have only to wait until then. I will present you with the heads of those you love.``
``...Ahahahahahaha, ahahahahahahaha!!``
``U-uwaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!!!``
Faced with that cruel and heartless verdict...
Ouka instinctively leapt to her feet, widened her eyes as she grabbed her sword, kicked off the ground, and swung.
...But the ``demon`` swatted away that desperate strike as if it were mere child's play, and sent her small body flying away.
Perhaps because she hit her head so hard... she couldn't stop her consciousness from sinking into the depths of the abyss.
...At this rate...
Is lying here all I can do... in the short time I have until I'm killed?
Without protecting the villagers like my parents?
Can I do nothing to stop Mother's violence...?!
``...! M-Mao-sama, we found one! It's a narrow path on a steep slope, but we should be able to get through it!``
``Good! Have our men take some ropes and climb up first!@k@vS16/00/DS43040257. Choose daredevils who won't flee and have them get as many villagers up as possible!``
``Y-yes ma'am!``
``Forgive us... Forgive us, Oyashiro-sama...!``
``Save us... Save us...! I don't want to die...!``
...I could see them.
The villagers desperately fighting to survive.
The faces of those dear to me... doing everything in their power to grasp their future.
...That's right.
It was the same... with me.
...It was the same for me, so I had to... I had to...!!
Stop her right here and now!!
...On your feet, Ouka Furude! Steel your heart and take up your sword!
Abandon your kindness and doubts... and become a demon!!
Turn your emotions, reasoning, and instincts into blades of ice and fight Mother... no, this ``demon``!!
Because I still have... precious people... I need to protect!!
``Aaaaaaaahh!!``
The ``demon`` turned around when it heard that shout akin to a roar... and faintly furrowed its brow as it stared ahead with a keen gaze.
There stood... a miserable, pitiful girl, barely holding her sword in one hand, her whole body wobbling.
``I-I won't let you go...!!@k@vS16/47/DS43010546. E-even if it costs me my life...!!``
The ``demon`` slowly turned to Ouka and raised its claws again.
Then... the moment its body appeared to sway...
``Then... I'll give you even more of a taste... of hell's agony!!``
The ``demon`` charged at Ouka and closed the distance in the blink of an eye.
Ouka drew her sword back a little... and put all her weight into her rear leg, standing at the ready with one foot forward.
...By this point, she was reaching her physical and mental limits.
So she no longer had time to hesitate or consider holding back...
Unless she slayed the ``demon`` right here and now... she would die, and even her loved ones would be killed...!
...She remembered what Hanyuu had once told her.
What swordsmanship required... was the constant resolve to harm your opponent.
``...What do you think is the easiest and most effective location to strike in a thrust?``
``Well... I would have to say the chest or the throat?``

``No.@k@vS16/45/DS43071946. When you attack someone, they will instinctively try to protect their vitals.@k@vS16/45/DS43071947. In that situation, the first thing they move... are their hands.@k@vS16/45/DS43071948. And naturally, their hands are what primarily moves when they attack.``
``...In other words, the hands are the body's attack and defense organs, and are always unconsciously moving based on what you're thinking.``

``However, in exchange, people aren't very conscious about protecting their own hands.@k@vS16/45/DS43071951. Thus, when they are attacked, their reaction when it comes to defending their hands is slower than anywhere else in the body.``
``But... I would think it'd be difficult to hit a moving target.``

``Even so, it's not easy for them to change their trajectories in the middle of an action, so it's easy to predict where they'll go.@k@vS16/45/DS43071953. So when thrusting, first go for the hands...``
``Raaaaaaaaaaaagh!!``
``Gwaaaaaaaaaagh?!``
The sword pierced through the ``demon's`` hand all the way to the other side of the blade.
A fierce spray of blood gushed out right then, staining the ``demon`` and Ouka's face crimson.
``...D-damn you...!@k@vS16/45/DS43071956. H-how dare you... Ah?!``
Right then.
The ``demon`` erupted with pain and hatred as it regained its stance, and turned its head with a roar...
...Only for its face to warp in surprise.
Shudders welled from its throat...
As it stared right at Ouka... standing in front of it.
...There was no mistaking it.
Ouka was in the pose for the strongest technique the ``demon`` had excelled in before it had lost its sanity.
She stuck out her left arm and pulled back her right elbow as if drawing a bow.
She held the sword perfectly level in one hand and placed her left palm on the sharp tip.
The blade was painted with speckles of fresh blood... and then...!
``Haah, haah... haah...!!``
``Haah... haah...``
``O-Ouka...!@k@vS16/45/DS43071960. Those eyes... those scarlet eyes are...!!``
Impossible.
It was impossible.
I remember her showing me that stance before... but it was nothing but a mere imitation back then.
I admit I couldn't help but admire her potential strength... but it was supposed to be a crude copy that would have never matched up to the real thing!
So why...?
How did Ouka know that?!
And... and that wasn't all!!
Why were her eyes... a deep crimson, as though they were bloodshot?!
Those crimson eyes... were supposed to indicate the worst and most violent sign that only a Ryuun could possess!! So why...?!
``I-it can't be...?!@k@vS16/45/DS43071962. Human... Ouka, you've ``demonified``?!``
``Mother...``
As for Ouka...
Her wild and turbulent panting gradually eased up, and her limbs, which had been shivering... ceased their shaking as if to match her breathing.
Her sword tip was pointed straight at the ``demon``.
The glint in her eye pierced the ``demon`` without the slightest tremor.
``Mother... no, ``demon``... I won't let you kill anyone else...!@k@vS16/47/DS43010557. I can never allow it!!``
That coldly shining resolution in her eyes...
Ouka's features... briefly overlapped with a familiar person's face, then vanished.
Those eyes... held a strong will in them, just like...
Those eyes were the spitting image of the person the ``demon`` had once loved and held dear...!
Would it have been for the best... if he and I hadn't met after all?
Was meeting him and marrying him... all a mistake?
And... and was giving birth to this child... what spawned this current hell...?!
The ``demon's`` heart rocked with regret, self-loathing, and despair... which mercilessly tormented it and tore it to bits.
...No! No, no, it wasn't! It couldn't be!!
I didn't want to accept it... Like hell I'd ever accept that those happy days were a mistake!!
And so... and so--!!
``Stop looking at me like thaaaaaat!!!``
With a roar like blood torn from its throat, the 'demon' charged straight at Ouka.
changeReason: Old uses idiomatic 'bloodcurdling roar'; new version 'roar like blood torn from its throat' more closely renders the Japanese 血を吐くような咆哮 (roar as if vomiting blood), preserving the visceral imagery.

TextENGNew: ...A tempest of godlike speed, too swift for the eye to follow.
changeReason: New translation captures 神速 (godlike speed) as 'godlike speed' whereas old version only says 'as fast as a gale', missing the supernatural connotation present in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: But Ouka clearly perceived every single movement...
``Raaaaaaaaaaaagh!!!``
Sensing Ouka was striking head on with all her might, the ``demon`` instantly changed its approach.
Ouka was aiming for the ``demon's`` undefended chest...
...No, its arm...!
(How naive...!!)
...Ouka had a tendency to always aim reliably when practicing.
She wouldn't let even the smallest of openings go, but would never strike if she sensed even the smallest possibility of a counterattack.
In theory, that wasn't wrong.
But this was an actual battle... A fight to the death.
If you didn't make full use of advantages you found and take the openings you'd created, there was no guarantee you'd get another chance to attack.
The resolve to make those decisions... was the decisive difference between Ouka and the ``demon``.
Gaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaagh!!!
The ``demon``, having instantly predicted Ouka's unconscious safety measure, forcefully twisted its hand as it swung down, changing its trajectory.
A moment later, Ouka's thrust vainly grazed through the empty air the ``demon`` would have originally struck.
``This is it! It's ov... Huh?!``
``Deyaaaaaaaaaaah!!``
But Ouka followed up by taking one step forward as she rammed her raised left arm against the back of her blade.
This caused the slowing blade strike to regain momentum and change from a thrust to a sideways slash, slamming hard into the disoriented ``demon's`` left arm and knocking it off balance.
Though its reaction to the surprise attack had been slow, the blow to the arm didn't even come close to wounding the ``demon``.
So, it filled its repelled arm with strength and brandished its claws right as it was about to swipe them down...
...It was astonished.
Ouka was at point blank distance to the ``demon``.
She'd pulled back her sword arm at some point, twisted her whole body like a spring as she stood in the Turuy Galkia stance again, and then...!!
``Farewell... Mother.``
``Teyaaaaaaaaaaaah!!``
``Gwaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaghh!!!``
The blade pierced the ``demon's`` chest... and struck all the way through its back.
Its limbs shivered as if it were convulsing, and a moment later...
``Gahaagh...!!``
It coughed up a massive spray of blood...
Why...?
Why wasn't I... born a human?
Why couldn't I... become a human?
Was defying fate my sin?
Was wishing for these feelings to be granted my sin?
Then... at the very least.
At the very least... I had to... Ouka...
Ouka mustered her remaining strength and returned to Furude Shrine to escape the raging flames.
But since she didn't have the courage to check the estate... she automatically headed further into the shrine grounds.
And what she found waiting for her at the elevated cliff that overlooked the village was...
A ruin... that was no longer the village Ouka knew.
The flames that had spread almost everywhere were burning as strong as ever, dyeing the area a sinister scarlet.
The moonlight illuminated wrecked houses and ravaged fields.
The stench of smoke rode the wind and spread all the way over to her.
The dirty dust clung to Ouka along with the heat.
...It was Hell.
What Ouka was witnessing was undoubtedly Hell.
Ouka shed tears and sobbed, unable to restrain her welling sorrow.
...It's my fault.
My foolish and shallow thoughts and actions hurt and devastated Onigafuchi Village and all its residents.
Those regrets and sins tormented Ouka and brought her to her knees.
Ouka had lost everything.
Her husband.
Her daughter.
And... her mother.
The only thing she had left to offer the lost lives and wounded people as compensation for her sin... was her meager and fragile body.
``My life is not enough to atone for my sins.@k@vS16/47/DS43010569. But...``
As she muttered that...
Ouka took the breaking sword she'd been using as a cane up until now and turned it around.
She then placed the tip to her neck... and took a deep breath.
...If I could turn back time...
Would I be able to tell my past self off for her shallow ideas, and tell her about the tragedy that awaited?
Tragedies are always caused by fools who don't know the future.
That's right. In the end, my powerlessness brought this on.
(...I'm sorry.)
I'm sorry to so many people.
Dearest Shouji.
Fuuka.
Shino-san.
Mao-san.
The villagers I scared.
All the people whose thoughts I clouded with greed.
And Father... and Mother.
Why...?
Why was I even born?
To bring disaster to this village?
Was I brought into this world to breed unnecessary greed and conflict?
If so, then was my birth a mistake to begin with...?
``At the very least, I want to apologize... to everyone in the Pure Land.@k@vS16/47/DS43010573. And... and this time, I'll... I'll...!``
Ouka muttered that as she closed her eyes... took her last breath...
Filled her sword with strength... and then...
``...Your death won't accomplish anything, will it...?``
Ouka's eyes widened, and she turned around in shock.
The bloodstained ``demon`` was standing there...
``Aah... aaaah...!!``
Ouka trembled as she hung her head, clasping her hands in dejection.
It wasn't unreasonable... She'd believed she'd confronted the ``demon`` with all her desperation and might, and finally slayed it once she'd found the resolve to stab it.
Yet that ``demon`` was still alive...?
I felt her despondency and despair in my skin as I slowly approached her.
And then...
``...I'm sorry, Ouka.``
Ouka gasped, raised her head, and looked up at me as I knelt down in front of her.
...Her pupils were no longer scarlet, but rather jet black like black quartz.
Once I saw that, I was able to rid myself of the last of my concerns and heaved a sigh of relief.
``Ouka...@k@vS16/45/DS43071979. It's my fault... that your gentle heart suffered... No apology can ever make up for that anymore.``
``But... I loved you.@k@vS16/45/DS43071981. I held you dearer than anyone else, and always wanted you to be happy...``
Ouka shed large tears... and sobbed when she heard that.
...She's back.
She came back to me.
My dear, gentle friend who always took care of me... And, my mother...
My eyes blurred again from the warmth of those heartfelt emotions.
But...
I no longer had the right to hug her.
And... most importantly... there was no time.
``...Mother?!``
``Don't worry about me...!!``
``I'm... going to die.@k@vS16/45/DS43071985. No, I must die... Ghh!!``
At that moment, I coughed up a sudden rush of blood.
(I have to hurry...)
I'd finally regained my sense of reason... but my injured body was rapidly regenerating... and I could feel my sense of self gradually breaking apart.
I'd completely turn into a ``demon`` at this rate. Once that happened, I'd attack humans in search of more blood and flesh, and become an avatar of tragedy.
There was no guarantee Ouka would be able to stop me... No, I'm sure she couldn't. She only had a little time left until her final chance to slay me was gone...!
(I mustn't leave any potential threats in this world...@k@vS16/45/DS43071988. Not as a Ryuun... and not as Ouka's mother...!)
...This soul and all these memories... had to be erased for all eternity.
That was why... I couldn't afford to die yet...! So that Ouka could completely cosign me into oblivion with her very hands...!!
``M-Mother...!``
``O-Ouka...@k@vS16/45/DS43071990. Please, listen to my final request...!@k@vS16/45/DS43071991. T-take this...``
And then, I...
Presented Ouka with what I'd brought from the depths of the ritual storehouse.
...``Ryuuou``, my trusted sword.
My irreplaceable partner... who had once saved my life and slain many enemies.
And now... it was the one thing that could end my life.
``Wh-what's that...?``
``...The sword that hunts Ryuun... hunts demons... ``Ryuuou``...@k@vS16/45/DS43071993. Use this... to strike me down...``
Because I kept my vow to live on man's land as a human, I was allowed to become Riku's wife... And I admonished myself.
So now that I'd broken that oath... I had to pay for that sin.
Atone for my sins, cosign the ``demons`` from this land to oblivion with that punishment... and bring peace back to the village.
That was the duty of the Furude family... I'd entrusted to Riku's father.
To kill me, I needed someone who also possessed Ryuun blood... In other words, it was only possible for members of the Furude family to slay me.

``Human swords can't kill Ryuun... that which you refer to as ``demons``.@k@vS16/45/DS43071995. But it's possible with my sword... with Ryuuou.@k@vS16/45/DS43071996. ...Use it.@k@vS16/45/DS43071997. And strike me down...!``
Ouka rejected it, shaking her head hard enough to dishevel her hair.
...I've lost everything.
Just when I thought I'd had nothing left, my mother came back to me.
And now... I was supposed to kill her again... with these hands?
I couldn't... I couldn't possibly do that!
``Ouka... Please.@k@vS16/45/DS43071999. You're the only one left... who can do this...!``
I urged myself to stay conscious as I desperately tried to convince Ouka.
I knew well just how kind Ouka was... and I was grateful for that. I'm sure I'd be crying tears of joy in any other circumstances.
But... that was no longer needed. It was futile...
``You may hate me...@k@vS16/45/DS43072001. You may hate me for saddling you with this blood, with this fate...``
``But even so... only one with Ryuun blood can kill a Ryuun...``
``So you're the only one I can ask...!``
``It is a child's duty... to bury and revere their parents.@k@vS16/45/DS43072005. You said that was the law of this world...@k@vS16/45/DS43072006. So obey that law and bury me...``

``Yes... Yes! I most certainly did say that...!@k@vS16/47/DS43010589. I know what I said, but that's why you're asking me to kill my mother and bury her?@k@vS16/47/DS43010590. There's no such law... I've never heard of such a law!``
``...I'm sorry. But please understand.``
And with those words...
I cut the mouth of Ryuuou's sheath and drew the sword to urge Ouka to make up her mind.
...The gleaming blade reflected my horns.
That single, utterly loathsome difference... had brought all this misery.
Even so... I hadn't passed them on to my daughter. That alone allowed me to remain thankful to God...
``Listen... Ouka...``
``I don't want to... I do not wish to listen!!``

``...People live with their sins.@k@vS16/45/DS43072014. ...And they can't live without making someone else take the blame.@k@vS16/45/DS43072015. They tell themselves that they're right... that they're not in the wrong... and try to find others to blame.``

``And nobody wants to take the blame, so they shift that responsibility around.@k@vS16/45/DS43072017. The symbol that emerged from that is a ``demon``.@k@vS16/45/DS43072018. ...The true demon disrupting the world of man.``
``So by bearing it myself, man may be cleansed, and mankind can be freed from their destiny of doubt and conflict...!``
Right... When you took it back to its root, this conflict started because of the rampage of a select few people who wallowed in greed.
There was indeed a part of me... that wanted them to be made aware of their sins. But put another way, that was my own way of trying to shift the blame.
So... they should no longer be blamed now that it had come to this. Any further censure and accusations of blame would likely split the village in two.
From now on... Onigafuchi Village would have to work together under Ouka and the others to create a peaceful land.
It was for that reason... that the cause of this tragedy should be entirely framed on the ``demon's`` rampage... so that no one would be blamed for their sins.
That was... my own form of redemption... to take responsibility for what I'd done...
So let all the sins, the filth, the karma, and the curse come to me.
Then attack me, purify me, wash me down the stream, and throw me in the swamp.
A human will not do. So it is the fate of one not human to accept that role.
That's right... If a human were made to bear it, then mankind would likely not be freed from the demon of doubt.
Then they would paint over good intentions with malice, doubt and hate others with nonexistent wicked hearts... and destroy one another.
I will be made an example of as the root of malice, to keep man from devolving into that worst-case scenario, and bring back their conscience...!
``I'm not a human of this world...@k@vS16/45/DS43072009. And my horns are proof that I'm a demon of this world. A demon's job is to take on the calamities of the human world.``

``Therefore, I will be killed as the source of the disaster that struck this village... and must purge the hearts of all those humans led astray by greed with my life...@k@vS16/45/DS43072011. That is my duty... and the reason... why I'm here...!``
``N-no... That doesn't make sense, that doesn't make sense!!@k@vS16/47/DS43010592. Why do you have to take on our sins all by yourself?!``
``People's sins are people's sins to begin with... Our sins are our own!!@k@vS16/47/DS43010594. They aren't yours!!@k@vS16/47/DS43010595. Isn't that right?!``
That's right.
This tragedy came about because I relied on my mother.
Much blood was spilled because I couldn't make up my mind before strife broke out.
It's not... Mother's fault.
It's mine... It's all my fault!!
Ouka said that and wept bitterly... wept for the likes of me.
She truly was... a kind girl.
She didn't live in hatred, she regretted and pondered her mistakes without averting her eyes from them... and then tried to improve on them.
That was why... Ouka needed to go on to lead the people.
And if it meant providing the cornerstone of her future... then I'd gladly become a ``demon``...
I'd take on all the sins and responsibility... bear them...
And then fade from this world as a ``demon``, the symbol of hatred and resentment.

``I don't understand... I don't understand, Mother!@k@vS16/47/DS43010599. Mother, you have horns.@k@vS16/47/DS43010600. Are you saying that you aren't human just because you have horns?@k@vS16/47/DS43010601. That even if you are human, the horns make you non-human?``
``That's... that's not right!@k@vS16/47/DS43010603. Horns or not, you're nothing but human to me!@k@vS16/47/DS43010604. ...You're not a demon or a monster... You're no monster, Mother!!``
``Ouka... thank you. You're the only one who's ever said that...``
Even if everyone else calls me demon or monster, you'll still call me human...
That's enough... That's all I need...
``...Please live.@k@vS16/45/DS43072022. Not just for yourself, but for your daughter... for those you hold dear.@k@vS16/45/DS43072023. That... is my wish.``
``N-no... Nooooo...!!``

``At this rate, I'll demonify again and lose my sense of reason...@k@vS16/45/DS43072025. Ouka... this is your last chance while I'm back to myself.@k@vS16/45/DS43072026. Please let me die... as your... mother...``
``M-Motheeer...!!``
``My life has been full of sad and painful memories...@k@vS16/45/DS43072028. I no longer know... how many times I've cried, suffered, and regretted.``

``But... meeting Riku, loving him...@k@vS16/45/DS43072030. and becoming your mother... are the only things I'll never regret.@k@vS16/45/DS43072031. They truly were... the happiest times of my life...``
``...I-it's the same for me...!``
Ouka reached out her trembling arms... finally took ``Ryuuou`` from my grasp, and then...
``...I too... was truly glad I was born your daughter... and got to spend all this time with you...!!``
Yes... this is all I need.
Now that I've heard Ouka say that... I can die.
Thank you, Ouka...
And... Riku.
I think I can finally go to you.
I may not have been a good mother...
But I'm sure this time... I'll be your good wife...
Eventually...
Ouka held ``Ryuuou`` at the ready... her tears showing no signs of stopping.
And then...
Although she shivered and hesitated over and over again...
She quietly pointed the tip at my heart, and got into a thrust stance...
``The moment you stab me... Chant the invocation after me.@k@vS16/45/DS43072034. Ready...?``
...Ouka.
I have one last wish after all the times I've messed with your life.
Please... live. And don't turn out like me.
...Yes, Mother.
I shall... put an end to the world that allows these sorts of conclusions right here and now.
And... I'll surely create a world where no one is hurt... where no one has to get hurt...!!
This was a fairytale from long, long ago.
A mysterious tale created from the smallest ``difference``.
The tale of the bonds of a family@k@vS16/00/VV_shino_1009. who lived earnestly for others@k@vS16/00/VV_shino_1010. and prayed earnestly for others.
...Morning had come.
Morning was impartially peaceful... even to the ravaged remains of Onigafuchi Village.
I opened my eyes.
I slowly rose to my feet and looked up to the sky.
...It was blue.
Radiant sunlight shone forth from the cloudless ultramarine sky onto the earth.
A clear breeze gently caressed my cheek.
Just then, I felt something faintly dry on my skin, and realized they were my tearstains.
If this were a dream...
If this were all just a bad dream...
I would thank God with all my heart and rejoice over the start of peaceful days.
But... this was reality.
The world I lived in. The world that couldn't be reset.
The sword sticking out of the ground that I used in place of a cane still had the branching blades sticking out of its sides... and was stained a brass color.
That shade... was unmistakably the color of her blood.
I wanted to believe that the fact it hadn't returned to its original form... was Mother's final wish for me to never use this sword again.
Just then...
I saw two people coming from the direction of the shrine.
One was small, the other was tall.
They were...
``...Mother?``
There was no way I could forget.
There was no way I could mistake them.
They were... my family.
My dear and beloved husband and daughter...!
``M-Mother...! Motheeeeer!!``
``Fuuka... Fuukaaa!!@k@vS16/47/DS43010619. Oh thank goodness... Thank goodness you're all right... Uuuu...!!``
``Ouka! You're badly wounded... are you okay?!``
``Yes... Yes!@k@vS16/47/DS43010621. You're alive too... Thank goodness... Thank goodness...!``
According to Shouji, the villagers who attacked him had assumed he was dead after his lung started bleeding and he stopped moving.
Likewise, Fuuka had been knocked unconscious from the savage beating and passed out... only to wake up a short while ago with her father in their living room that had survived the fire.
And...
``...O-Ouka?!@k@vS16/00/DS43040259. Is that you, Ouka?!``
``...Thank goodness... Everyone's alive...!@k@vS16/00/DS43030436. Ouka... Oukaaaaa!!``
Her loved ones rejoiced and cried... as they ran over.
When she saw that... Ouka smiled.
Sad, painful, and harsh memories that made her want to abandon everything weighed heavy on her small shoulders...
But even so, she found herself smiling when she saw everyone...
I've lost many things.
I've let go of more happiness than I can begin to count.
I want to always protect this happiness, and everything I hold dear.
And I want to create a world where sins are no longer wiped away through sacrifice.
People joining hands, cooperating, helping one another in a spirit of mutual aid... That was the utopia our mother dreamed of.
I would create it upon this land, without fail.
And one day, when our mother was reborn into this world, I would make this a village where she could greet it with a smile.
changeReason: Old version says Mother can 'come to that ideal village with a smile'; new version says the speaker will 'make this a village where she could greet it with a smile,' which more accurately reflects the Japanese emphasis on the speaker's agency in transforming the village.

TextENGNew: That is the destiny of those born into the Furude family... into the Three Families. --No.
It is the duty I must fulfill as the one who inherited the blood of our mother -- the one who truly wished to love this land and its people...
``--Mother.@k@vS16/47/DS43010623. I will live on.@k@vS16/47/DS43010624. As long as there are those I hold dear, I will live on with all my strength--``
changeReason: Old 'As long as those I love need me' captures 求めてくれる (as long as they seek/need me); new 'As long as there are those I hold dear' shifts the meaning from being needed by others to simply having dear ones, losing the reciprocal nuance.

TextENGNew: With those words spoken silently in my heart, as if calling out to the one I could no longer see, I gazed up at the heavens.
changeReason: New translation adds 'with those words spoken silently in my heart' which translates そう心の中で from the Japanese, a detail the old translation omitted entirely.

TextENGNew: And then... Fuuka's small hand gently, tenderly enfolded mine.
The warm dream had ended, and cold reality had begun.
changeReason: Old translates あたたかな夢 as 'blissful dream' while new uses 'warm dream,' which is the literal meaning; 'warm' and 'blissful' evoke different emotional registers.

TextENGNew: But the light... fell equally upon all things.
